title: Import a coded-long CSV and fit HB-MNL via CmdStan NUTS sampling
summary: Import a coded-long CSV, auto-generate a Stan JSON data file, and run HB-MNL sampling via CmdStan NUTS. Handles data conversion automatically and saves stan_data.json in run_dir for reproducibility. Follow with finalize to compute diagnostics and part-worth exports.
description: Reads a coded long-format choice CSV (one row per respondent-task-alternative), converts it to the Stan JSON data layout expected by the HB-MNL model, and runs CmdStan NUTS sampling. This is the primary entry point for fitting the Stan-based hierarchical Bayes model: it saves both the intermediate stan_data.json and the per-chain CmdStan CSV output in run_dir, making the entire pipeline reproducible. Follow with finalize to compute Rhat, ESS diagnostics, and the posterior-mean part-worth matrix.
usage:
[["hb-stan::fit"]]
input = "<coded_long.csv>"
run_dir = "<dir>"
run_name = "stan_run"
# optional:
headers = true
num_samples = 1000
num_warmup = 500
num_chains = 4
seed = <int>
thin = <int>
parallel_chains = true
prior_lkj_eta = 1.0
sigma_scale = 1.0
ridge_scale = 1e-6

arguments.name: input
arguments.description: Path to the coded long-format CSV. Required columns: resp_id, task_id, alt_id, x1, ..., xP, y. Rows must be sorted ascending by (resp_id, task_id, alt_id). y is the choice weight: positive for the chosen alternative(s), 0 for unchosen. Same column layout as model-hb-mh.

arguments.name: run_dir
arguments.description: Output directory. stan_data.json and chain CSV files (hb_fit_chain_1.csv, ...) are written here. Created if it does not exist.

arguments.name: run_name
arguments.description: Short identifier for this run. Non-empty string. Used to name output artifacts and subdirectories so multiple runs under the same run_dir do not collide. Must not contain path separators or '..'.

arguments.name: headers
arguments.description: Whether the CSV has a header row. When true, attribute names are read from the header and carried into stan_data.json and the finalize outputs. Default true.

arguments.name: num_samples
arguments.description: Post-warmup draws per chain. Total posterior draws = num_samples × num_chains. Default 1000. Increase for better posterior precision at the cost of file size.

arguments.name: num_warmup
arguments.description: Warmup (adaptation) iterations per chain. CmdStan's NUTS sampler tunes the step size and mass matrix during warmup; these draws are discarded. Default 500.

arguments.name: num_chains
arguments.description: Number of independent chains. At least 4 is recommended for reliable Rhat and ESS diagnostics. Default 4.

arguments.name: seed
arguments.description: RNG seed. Each chain receives seed + (chain_index − 1) so chains are independent but reproducible. Omit for a non-deterministic seed.

arguments.name: thin
arguments.description: Thinning factor: keep every Nth draw. Reduces output file size at the cost of effective sample size. Omit to keep all draws.

arguments.name: parallel_chains
arguments.description: Run chains in parallel using available CPU threads up to num_chains. Set false for serial execution to reduce peak memory at the cost of wall time. Default true.

arguments.name: prior_lkj_eta
arguments.description: LKJ prior shape parameter η for the population-level correlation matrix. η = 1 is uniform over correlations; η > 1 shrinks toward identity (reduces implied between-attribute correlation). Default 1.0.

arguments.name: sigma_scale
arguments.description: Prior scale for individual-level standard deviations σ (half-normal prior). Larger values allow wider spread of part-worths across respondents. Default 1.0.

arguments.name: ridge_scale
arguments.description: Ridge scale for the covariance prior. Small values (default 1e-6) keep the prior weakly informative without distorting the posterior.

details:
`fit` is the primary HB-Stan estimation entry point. It converts the coded-long CSV into the Stan JSON data format (saving stan_data.json for reproducibility), then invokes CmdStan's NUTS sampler on the HB-MNL model. The model places a multivariate normal prior on the population-level mean partworths with an LKJ prior on the correlation matrix, and per-respondent partworths are drawn from this population distribution.

The NUTS sampler adapts its step size and mass matrix during warmup and then draws from the posterior during the sampling phase. With the defaults (4 chains × 1000 post-warmup draws) you get 4000 total posterior draws, which is sufficient for most conjoint studies. Increase num_samples if the posterior summary shows low ESS values.

After sampling completes, run `finalize` to compute per-parameter Rhat and ESS diagnostics, posterior summaries, and the U × P part-worth matrix. `fit-finalize` runs both steps in one call if you don't need to inspect the raw chains first.
value:
On success, run_dir contains:

- stan_data.json — the converted Stan data file (reproducible input for `sample`).
- hb_fit_chain_1.csv, hb_fit_chain_2.csv, ... — CmdStan CSV files, one per chain, containing the raw posterior draws.

In protocol mode the plugin emits event.run.started, periodic event.run.progress events as chains complete, and a final event.run.finished (or event.run.failed).
notes: Rows must be sorted by (resp_id, task_id, alt_id) — the Stan data conversion assumes this ordering.
Use fit-finalize to run sampling and finalization in one step when you don't need to inspect raw chains.
For cross-validation, use cv-fit instead of calling fit once per fold — it walks a choice-cv split directory and manages fold-level seeds automatically.
CmdStan must be installed and on PATH for this command to work.

see_also.description: Compute diagnostics and part-worth exports from the chain CSVs written here.
Convenience wrapper that runs fit then finalize in one call.
Cross-validation entry point — runs fit across a choice-cv split directory.
Low-level entry point that accepts a pre-built Stan JSON data file.
Produces the coded long-format CSV that this command consumes.
MH-based HB alternative — no CmdStan dependency, supports mixture segments and covariates.

examples.title: Longer run with reproducible seed and more warmup
examples.body:
[["hb-stan::fit"]]
input = "./estimation/coded_long.csv"
run_dir = "./runs/hb-stan-long"
run_name = "stan_run2"
num_samples = 2000
num_warmup = 1000
seed = 42

examples.title: Conservative prior with tighter LKJ and reduced sigma
examples.body:
[["hb-stan::fit"]]
input = "./estimation/coded_long.csv"
run_dir = "./runs/hb-stan-conservative"
run_name = "stan_run3"
prior_lkj_eta = 2.0
sigma_scale = 0.5
seed = 1

examples.title: Serial chains to reduce peak memory on a large study
examples.body:
[["hb-stan::fit"]]
input = "./estimation/coded_long.csv"
run_dir = "./runs/hb-stan-serial"
run_name = "stan_run4"
num_chains = 4
parallel_chains = false

fields.name: input
fields.description: Path to the coded-long CSV (resp_id, task_id, alt_id, x1..xP, y).

fields.name: run_dir
fields.description: Output directory for stan_data.json and chain CSVs.

fields.name: run_name
fields.description: Short identifier for this run. Non-empty string. Used to name output artifacts and subdirectories so multiple runs under the same run_dir do not collide. Must not contain path separators or '..'.

fields.name: headers
fields.description: Whether the CSV has a header row. Default: true.

fields.name: thin
fields.description: Keep every Nth draw. Omit to keep all.

fields.name: parallel_chains
fields.description: Run chains in parallel. Default: true.

fields.name: prior_lkj_eta
fields.description: LKJ prior shape parameter η. Default: 1.0.

fields.name: sigma_scale
fields.description: Half-normal prior scale for individual σ. Default: 1.0.

fields.name: ridge_scale
fields.description: Ridge scale for covariance prior. Default: 1e-6.
